title: The Yellow Badge Of Cowardge

jokes.text: D'oh! (Homer's signature frustrated exclamation)
jokes.setup: Opening sequence chaos
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic catchphrase in response to mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment, perfectly integrated character comedy but predictable

jokes.text: My brother is about to meet fear... And as usual... He won't pass.
jokes.setup: Lisa quoting Buddha about passing beyond fear
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Bart failing at everything as usual
jokes.explanation: Smart juxtaposition of Buddhist wisdom with Bart's character flaws

jokes.text: Found my summer hobby! Banging pots together!
jokes.setup: Bart excitedly announcing the last day of school
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous choice of summer activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's chaotic energy and simple pleasures

jokes.text: That's a prison road crew. I did that last summer. I'm not allowed back.
jokes.setup: Homer enrolling Bart in 'fresh air summer camp'
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's prison labor and Homer's casual criminal past
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation, perfect Homer character beat

jokes.text: Needs more numerators.
jokes.setup: Homer drinking what appears to be a mathematical smoothie
jokes.punchline: Treating math terms like food ingredients
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Homer's mathematical ignorance

jokes.text: The fire department has to raise money by delivering pizza.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about no fireworks due to city being broke
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of firefighters moonlighting as pizza delivery
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical image of municipal budget crisis

jokes.text: The fourth of July is the one day a year when our city puts on her high heels and tube top and leans into america's car window.
jokes.setup: Homer's patriotic speech about keeping up appearances
jokes.punchline: Comparing the city to a prostitute soliciting America
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor combining patriotism with prostitution, very quotable

jokes.text: He actually has three legs. You lay off my son tripod! He won fair and square, just like my twins wheel and barrow.
jokes.setup: Field day egg race competition
jokes.punchline: Kids named after mobility aids dominating physical competition
jokes.explanation: Clever naming joke with perfect escalation from tripod to wheelbarrow

jokes.text: I like the play-doh that comes out the back.
jokes.setup: Face painting activity at field day
jokes.punchline: Ralph's disturbing misunderstanding of basic bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph innocent weirdness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Previous winners include Sideshow Mel, Señora Bumblebee Man and Olympian Edwin Moses.
jokes.setup: Announcing the climactic race around school
jokes.punchline: Real Olympian competing against elementary school kids
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation mixing cartoon characters with real athlete

jokes.text: Milhouse revealing his 'dummy tummy' training secret
jokes.setup: Milhouse claims he's been secretly training
jokes.punchline: His training was just wearing a fake fat belly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Milhouse's naive optimism and weak planning

jokes.text: Breaking that tape cured your asthma. (deep voice): Marvelous! Ha-ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Milhouse winning the race despite being weak
jokes.punchline: Sudden dramatic voice change suggesting miraculous transformation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with voice change, decent execution

jokes.text: Milhouse?! That's a thousand-to-one odds.
jokes.setup: Betting on the school race
jokes.punchline: Even the mob sees Milhouse as hopeless
jokes.explanation: Standard underdog joke, fits Fat Tony character

jokes.text: I ain't makin' book no more. I lost the deed to my shack!
jokes.setup: Students looking for someone to take their bets
jokes.punchline: Willie's gambling problem has cost him his home
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Willie's perpetual bad luck and poor decisions.

jokes.text: Gun in the school! What the...? Just, just go.
jokes.setup: Starting pistol fired for race
jokes.punchline: Panic about gun in school, then casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about school safety, good timing

jokes.text: Did you come to give me a cup of water?
jokes.setup: Milhouse leading the race, Bart approaches
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's innocent trust before Bart betrays him
jokes.explanation: Effective dramatic irony, audience knows betrayal is coming

jokes.text: The best day of my life just turned into every other day of my life.
jokes.setup: Milhouse getting beaten up after leading race
jokes.punchline: Philosophical acceptance of his consistently terrible luck
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Milhouse's perpetual disappointment

jokes.text: Lisa's narrator comparison: 'like he did with every multiple choice question he ever saw, Bart chose B'
jokes.setup: Bart faced choice between helping friend or running away
jokes.punchline: Comparing moral choice to his test-taking strategy
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary linking Bart's academic laziness to moral cowardice

jokes.text: Lisa! Bath time! Mom! I'm narrating! The water's gonna get cold! Fine.
jokes.setup: Lisa providing dramatic narration
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall interrupted by mundane parental demands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment breaking narrative convention with domestic reality

jokes.text: Now I've got to look at some severe cases of ice cream headache. (theme from M*A*S*H* playing)
jokes.setup: Doctor treating injured children from field day
jokes.punchline: Trivial injuries treated like wartime trauma with M*A*S*H theme
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference contrasting serious war drama with minor injuries

jokes.text: Eh, I've had my problems with sparklers. Fun! Fun! Fun! (screams)
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting sparklers as safe alternative
jokes.punchline: Homer's traumatic sparkler injury flashback
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer getting hurt by seemingly safe things

jokes.text: Are you god? No, no. But I shoot rockets into his a-face.
jokes.setup: Young Homer in awe of fireworks operator
jokes.punchline: Giuseppe's blasphemous description of his profession
jokes.explanation: Bold irreverent humor with great economy and memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Well, I am younger than Sylvester Stallone. I will do it!
jokes.setup: Homer's Expendables reference about old people doing their best work
jokes.punchline: Giuseppe's age comparison as motivation
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Will you marry me? Meow, meow, maybe. Why would a kitten marry a crocodile? Wouldn't it eat her? Suspension of disbelief.
jokes.setup: Watching children's show about animal romance
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical objection dismissed with pretentious term
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between logical thinking and fantasy acceptance

jokes.text: Milhouse only being able to handle baby show 'Cassidy the Crocodile' after his beating
jokes.setup: Milhouse invites Bart over to watch TV
jokes.punchline: His trauma has regressed him to only tolerating toddler programming
jokes.explanation: Good character consequence showing lasting effects of trauma

jokes.text: Crystal meth? Then what the heck did I sell to them Colombian drug lords?
jokes.setup: Discovering gunpowder has crystal meth in it
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of drug dealing with international cartels
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation fitting Cletus's criminal hillbilly character

jokes.text: Rather than try to figure out what happened, Milhouse had the courage to move on. To me, that's a hero.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to make amends publicly
jokes.punchline: Praising amnesia/avoidance as heroism
jokes.explanation: Clever irony about redefining cowardice as courage

jokes.text: Lisa narrating 'Bart knew the worst thing to do was pile lie upon lie. And that's exactly what he did.'
jokes.setup: Moral lesson about honesty
jokes.punchline: Immediate statement that Bart will do the opposite
jokes.explanation: Good setup-subversion structure showing Bart's predictable poor choices

jokes.text: Stop the tattoo! Uh, I could make it a bag of groceries. All right, make sure there's some French bread sticking out.
jokes.setup: Crowd angry at Bart's cowardice, stopping commemorative tattoo
jokes.punchline: Practical improvisation to salvage botched tattoo work
jokes.explanation: Absurd but logical solution to awkward situation, good visual comedy

jokes.text: Well, join the club. Anyone who makes it to old age has got to be part-coward.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining everyone calls him a coward
jokes.punchline: Grampa's cynical life wisdom about survival
jokes.explanation: Dark wisdom perfectly fitting Grampa's war veteran cynicism

jokes.text: After Korea, I was drowning in boobies.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining benefits of cowardice outliving heroes
jokes.punchline: Crude description of romantic success with war widows
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate humor about war's aftermath

jokes.text: This is an angry sleepover. I'm only doing it 'cause it was on the books.
jokes.setup: Kids refusing to have sleepover with coward Bart
jokes.punchline: Nelson's reluctant contractual obligation to bully
jokes.explanation: Funny bureaucratic approach to childhood social dynamics

jokes.text: Wrong holiday, Charlie Brown.
jokes.setup: Homer humming Christmas song during July 4th fireworks prep
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference to Peanuts holiday confusion
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much creativity

jokes.text: Giuseppe's 'Wrong holiday, Charlie Brown' response to 'Sometimes When We Touch'
jokes.setup: Romantic song playing during fireworks preparation
jokes.punchline: Reference to wrong Peanuts holiday special
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference but not particularly clever execution

jokes.text: Actually, July 2 is when congress declared independence, the date that-a should be celebrated. Hey, super mario. Don't you tell me about america!
jokes.setup: Giuseppe correcting American history
jokes.punchline: Homer's xenophobic rejection of accurate information
jokes.explanation: Good satire of American ignorance and xenophobia

jokes.text: I was just tweeting a picture of what I thought was my last meal. Hot dog and french fries!
jokes.setup: Someone asking who the hero is during fireworks disaster
jokes.punchline: Carl's mundane social media priorities during crisis
jokes.explanation: Decent social media satire but not particularly original

jokes.text: 'Quit explaining everything, let him talk' to the over-narrating announcer
jokes.setup: Announcer over-explaining the rescue situation
jokes.punchline: Crowd member calling out the exposition
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on TV tropes but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Look, Maggie, I undid my deed. So I'm just gonna take your feather and say good night. Oh, where are these coming from?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to end his coward nightmares by removing chicken feather
jokes.punchline: More feathers appearing, suggesting deeper psychological issue
jokes.explanation: Good callback to cowardice theme with escalating visual gag

jokes.text: One more year, tops. I live-a longer than you. I take a senior spin class. Always I'm increasing the tension.
jokes.setup: Romantic song about holding someone till death
jokes.punchline: Morbid competition over who dies first, Giuseppe's exercise boasting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mortality with character-appropriate competitive banter

tmdb_overview: Bart feels guilty after he wins the annual "last day of school" race around Springfield Elementary School, with help from Nelson, who beats up the frontrunner, Milhouse. Meanwhile, Homer tries to bring back 4th of July fireworks after they are canceled due to budget cuts.
